findings.message: Falsy check `if not item:` is the wrong idiom for testing whether `session.get()` returned a row. SQLAlchemy ORM instances do not override `__bool__`, so the check happens to work today, but it would silently return HTTP 404 for a legitimately-found item if the model ever adds a `__bool__` or `__len__` method (e.g. via a mixin or future column). The comment in the diff itself acknowledges the bug: a `0`-valued primary key is a valid edge case that exposes the fragility of truthy/falsy object evaluation.
findings.suggestion: Replace `if not item:` with `if item is None:` — the identity check is the only semantically correct test for 'session.get() found no row'.
